claim.content:
Under the simplifying assumption that the Landau Fermi-liquid parameter F_{2}=0, the shear-viscosity-to-relaxation-time ratio in normal liquid 3He satisfies the Fermi-liquid relation
      $$
      \frac{\eta}{\tau_{\eta}}=\frac{1}{5}\rho p_{F}^{2}\frac{1}{(m^{*}/m)m^{2}},
      $$
      where η is the shear viscosity, τ_{\eta} the shear relaxation time, ρ the mass density, p_{F}=\hbar(3\pi^{2}n)^{1/3} the Fermi momentum with number density n=\rho/m, m the bare particle mass, and m^{*} the quasiparticle effective mass; this relation is invoked to convert measured viscoelastic quantities into an estimate of the effective-mass ratio m^{*}/m.
      [6]
